title: To Know There Is a God
description: Recognize and affirm the existence of the Creator.

title: Do Not Entertain Other Deities
description: Do not worship or accept any power as divine besides God.

title: Proclaim Divine Unity
hebrew: שמע ישראל ה׳ אלהינו ה׳ אחד
description: Declare the oneness of God through the Shema.

title: Love God
description: Cultivate deep love for God through thought, feeling, and action.

title: Fear and Revere God
description: Hold reverence for God and avoid spiritual arrogance.

title: Sanctify God's Name
description: Act in ways that honor and sanctify God's name publicly.

title: Do Not Profane God's Name
description: Avoid behavior that dishonors God's name.

title: Pray to God
description: Serve God through heartfelt prayer.
notes: Traditional source for daily prayer obligation

title: Recite the Shema Morning and Night
description: Recite the Shema in the evening and in the morning.

title: Priestly Blessing
description: Kohanim are commanded to bless the people of Israel.

title: Study Torah
description: Engage continually in Torah learning.

title: Teach Torah to Children
description: Parents are commanded to transmit Torah to the next generation.

title: Honor Torah Scholars
description: Show respect for elders and Torah wisdom.

title: Write a Torah Scroll
hebrew: ועתה כתבו לכם את השירה הזאת
description: Ensure Torah is preserved through written transmission.
notes: Traditionally linked to commissioning or writing a scroll

title: Rest on Shabbat
description: Cease creative labor on the seventh day.

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on Shabbat
description: Refrain from melakhah on Shabbat.

title: Sanctify Shabbat with Words
description: Mark Shabbat with verbal sanctification such as Kiddush.

title: Do Not Leave One's Place for Gathering Manna
hebrew: אל יצא איש ממקמו ביום השביעי
description: A classic source tied to Shabbat boundaries and restraint.
notes: Interpreted within laws of Shabbat movement

title: Rest on the First Day of Passover
description: Observe sacred assembly and rest on the opening day of Passover.

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on Passover's First Day
description: Avoid prohibited labor on the first festival day.

title: Eat Matzah on Passover Night
description: Eat unleavened bread at the Passover seder.

title: Do Not Eat Chametz on Passover
description: Refrain from consuming leaven during Passover.

title: Remove Chametz Before Passover
description: Eliminate leaven from one's possession before Passover.
notes: Basis for bedikat and biur chametz

title: Hear the Shofar on Rosh Hashanah
description: Mark the new year through the sounding of the shofar.

title: Afflict Oneself on Yom Kippur
description: Practice fasting and self-reflection on the Day of Atonement.

title: Do Not Eat or Drink on Yom Kippur
hebrew: כי כל הנפש אשר לא תענה
description: Abstain from eating and drinking on Yom Kippur.

title: Dwell in a Sukkah
description: Live in a sukkah during the festival of Sukkot.
notes: Remembers divine protection in the wilderness

title: Take the Four Species
description: Take lulav, etrog, hadasim, and aravot on Sukkot.

title: Honor Father and Mother
description: Treat parents with honor through speech and action.

title: Revere Father and Mother
description: Show reverence and avoid disrespect to parents.

title: Be Fruitful and Multiply
description: Build family life and continue human partnership with creation.

title: Do Not Murder
description: Human life is sacred and must not be taken unlawfully.

title: Do Not Steal
description: Respect the property and dignity of others.

title: Do Not Bear False Witness
description: Testify truthfully and avoid harmful false testimony.

title: Do Not Covet
description: Do not scheme to acquire what belongs to another.
notes: Addresses inward desire and outward action

title: Love Your Neighbor as Yourself
description: Act toward others with empathy, dignity, and care.

title: Do Not Take Revenge
description: Refrain from retaliatory behavior.

title: Do Not Bear a Grudge
description: Let go of ongoing resentment against others.

title: Leave the Corner of the Field for the Poor
description: Set aside agricultural produce for those in need.

title: Leave Gleanings for the Poor
description: Permit the needy to gather leftover produce.

title: Give Charity
description: Open your hand generously to those in need.

title: Do Not Harden Your Heart Against the Poor
description: Do not ignore or close yourself to poverty around you.

title: Inspect Signs of Kosher Animals
description: Eat land animals that meet Torah criteria for kashrut.

title: Do Not Eat Non-Kosher Animals
description: Refrain from consuming species prohibited by Torah.

title: Do Not Eat Blood
description: Blood is forbidden for consumption.
notes: Applies to domesticated and wild animals

title: Judge Cases Justly
description: Apply law fairly without bias.

title: Do Not Pervert Justice
description: Do not distort legal outcomes.

title: Appoint Judges and Officers
description: Establish systems of governance and justice.

title: Build a Sanctuary
description: Create a sacred place dedicated to divine service.

title: Guard the Sanctuary
description: Maintain reverence and protective oversight of holy space.
